claim: Agentic layer is new ring around codebase where agents operate applications, consisting of memory files, prime commands, skills, MCP servers, and specialized prompts
actionable: Structure agentic layer: agent.md memory files + prime commands (activation prompts) + skills/MCPs (tools) + specialized prompts per concern
source_title: The Codebase Singularity: “My agents run my codebase better than I can”

claim: Class 1 Grade 3 agentic layer adds custom tools (skills, MCP servers, tool-access prompts) enabling agents to interact with external systems
actionable: At Class 1 Grade 3, expose tools via skills (simple scripts), MCP servers (complex integrations), or prompt engineering
source_title: The Codebase Singularity: “My agents run my codebase better than I can”

claim: Class 1 Grade 4 adds closed-loop prompts with feedback loops (plan → build → review → fix) enabling self-correcting agents that resolve their own work
actionable: Implement closed-loop prompts: plan task, build implementation, have agent review, fix issues iteratively until done
source_title: The Codebase Singularity: “My agents run my codebase better than I can”

claim: Codebase singularity achieved when engineers trust agents to run codebase better than themselves, nothing ships without agent approval
actionable: Build toward singularity via grades: memory + prompts → add tools → add closed loops → add parallelization
source_title: The Codebase Singularity: “My agents run my codebase better than I can”
